content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Count the number of distinct arrays DDD of size NNN containing non-negative integers such that it is possible to construct a graph GGG of NNN nodes with the following constraints:


For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), node iii has degree DiD_iDi​ in GGG.
D1+D2+…+DN=2ND_1 + D_2 + \ldots + D_N = 2ND1​+D2​+…+DN​=2N.
GGG is simple, connected, and **bipartite**.
This value can be large, so compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain a single integer NNN - the size of the array DDD.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer: the number of distinct arrays DDD satisfying the given conditions, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: Note that the sum of NNN across all tests is **not bounded.**
